proposed_codes.name: report-progress
proposed_codes.definition: Human reports completion of an off-session manual action (Zotero edit, BBT export, upstream patch) that gates the AI's next pipeline step, carrying no other directive signal. Distinct from assign-task: the load is the unblocking status, not a new instruction.
proposed_codes.example_quote: Okay. I've added it and updated the BBT. Now we need to migrate, mathpix, etc.

proposed_codes.name: import-external-pattern
proposed_codes.definition: Human brings a structure, workflow, or artifact pattern from another project/repo and directs the AI to adapt it to the current work.
proposed_codes.example_quote: I want you to investigate how the manuscript for ...SAMPLE-STRATEGY-ECD. Notice how there was a json file with multiple levels ... We want to mimic that.

proposed_codes.name: system-artifact
proposed_codes.definition: A kept turn whose text is machine-emitted (task-completion notification, command stdout, tool callback) carrying no authored human intent; coded only because it occupies a turn slot.
proposed_codes.example_quote:
<status>completed</status>
<summary>Background command "Run analytic reproduction solo (single-process)" completed (exit code 0)</summary>

proposed_codes.name: system-notification
proposed_codes.definition: A turn that is an automated task-completion or command-stdout notification injected into the user channel, carrying no human-authored move.
proposed_codes.example_quote: Background command "Run brute-force pin of E[A],E[B],E[T] in background" completed (exit code 0)

proposed_codes.name: withhold-go-ahead
proposed_codes.definition: The human asks a clarifying or scoping question while explicitly forbidding the AI from acting on it yet (information-only, no execution).
proposed_codes.example_quote: So basically we just need to run the full battery again? Don’t do it. Just asking

proposed_codes.name: monitor-background-job
proposed_codes.definition: The human (or the harness on the human's behalf) surfaces a completed background task notification, keeping a long-running compute/verification job under supervision without issuing a new instruction.
proposed_codes.example_quote: <summary>Background command "Phase-8 committed evidence run (R=40000, 28 cores)" completed (exit code 0)</summary>

proposed_codes.name: request-handoff
proposed_codes.definition: Asks the AI to package the current state into a resumable artifact (resume prompt, fresh-session todo, status doc) for a future session or person.
proposed_codes.example_quote: Write a todo list that I can take to a fresh session

proposed_codes.name: drive-tooling-pipeline
proposed_codes.definition: Human personally operates external tooling steps (Zotero migrate, convert, index, BBT export, RAG/db deletes) the AI cannot do, gating the AI's next action on completion signals.
proposed_codes.example_quote: I'm converting them and indexing them now. I'll let you know when to lit-sync tools should be invoked

proposed_codes.name: system-notification
proposed_codes.definition: A non-human-authored turn injected by the harness (task-completion, monitor-timeout, command stdout) that carries no human intent and is present only because it sat in the user-turn stream.
proposed_codes.example_quote: Background command "Full precise R MC + assembly run" completed (exit code 0)

session_summaries.summary: This 2026-06-01 session derived the closed-form general-MVN (A)+(C) Term-A asymptote, with the hard ingredient being the second-order FIML bias of the covariance under MAR. Marcus's role was to set the agenda (literature-first, don't reinvent the wheel), gate the derivation through a plan, steer the work to manuscript form, probe method choices (why finite differencing when analytic Hessians exist), handle Zotero acquisitions himself, and request a clean handoff prompt for the next session. Much of the human-side traffic is short scheduling/status checks while long ampute-bound Monte Carlo runs completed in the background.

The inception session: Marcus loaded project context, then drove a long literature-acquisition pipeline (Zotero cleanup, Mathpix conversion, RAG indexing, citation-discipline infrastructure) while reading Chan/Chan-Meng (2022). Mid-session the discovery that Chan (2022, AoS) preempts the original spectral chi-square framing forces a reframing toward the deviance-bias decomposition with LRT and IC applications. Marcus's role was directive and corrective throughout: setting verification/citation discipline, supplying domain knowledge (FMI invertibility, missing-covariate citations, MI-IC simulation results), rejecting overreach (the anisotropy term, uncited papers), and steering the venue/style/contribution decisions.

This 2026-06-06 session designed and stood up the manuscript plan system. Marcus directed the AI to investigate an exemplar repo's multi-level JSON plan, then steered the design toward a strategic, multi-session human-AI collaboration apparatus (project manager, citation-discipline hook, level-4 evidence map), and ratified the IMRaD-with-Methods-as-workflow restructuring as D-09. The human's role was almost entirely architectural and directive: setting the agenda, importing a model from another project, insisting on specific safeguards, and locking the structural decision.

A single-turn session in which Marcus commissions the build of the /derivation-audit project skill: a read-only flag-only auditor that mechanizes the project's verification and citation-discipline standards over manuscript/derivation.qmd. His role was to fully specify the audit's standards (citation backing as BLOCKER, evidence-to-external-exact-text matching against literature/ files, reproducible-artifact pointers, [mc]-to-assert pairing, over-claiming and house-jargon detection) and to mandate grounding every check in the repo's real conventions plus a test run.

Marcus directed a focused session to reconcile the MAR design-imbalance term (A)+(C) by settling the information convention (observed vs expected) and deriving the realized-information correction two independent ways (Claude analytic + a blind GPT-5.5 cross-model check), then de-provisionalizing @sec-termA. His role was to set the verification bar (cross-model agreement, blind packets), commission an independent fresh-session audit, then enforce the audit's prose-honesty fixes (no over-claiming 'two ways agree' or the -0.22+Delta=-0.46 trap), and finally spec a handoff that scopes 'fully derived' to a later session while standing up a cross-model derivation protocol.

On 2026-06-02 Marcus directed the assistant to execute the approved derivation-audit remediation plan (W1-W5 + lifecycle/jargon fixes to manuscript/derivation.qmd), then pivoted to the harder question of getting multi-source evidence that the novel decomposition is correct. He supplied the Wolfram client library lead, installed Mathematica to add a second independent CAS, and queued two plans for fresh sessions: independent adversarial re-derivation (todo/013) and the non-monotone Cox-Snell pilot (todo/014). His role was to set the verification standard, contribute the second-CAS resource, partition scope across sessions, and gate every commit.

An overnight-into-next-day manuscript-planning marathon: Marcus reopened Level-1 review, walked the five-move IMRaD arc (M1-M5) and ratified a six-move version (D-09 amendment, D-10 replication principle), then drove Level-2 and the from-scratch drafting of S1/S2/S3 in plain prose. The human's role was relentless framing and prose adjudication: typing 'unresolved' claims as asserted-vs-documented, demanding the proper-vs-improper imputation distinction, rejecting poetic cadence three times, and specifying the qualitative collaboration analysis (todo/032) with D-13.

An adversarial verification session: Marcus commissioned an independent audit of the AI-assisted (A)+(C) MAR information-convention resolution (todo/018, commit d37183b), explicitly forbidding trust in the commit message or prior summaries and demanding per-claim refutation from literature, code, and reproductions. The human's role was to design and enforce the verification protocol (adversarial stance, blind cross-model independence checks, citation/render gates) and then to request the fixes be packaged as a portable correction prompt. The audit returned PASS with two material WARNs (a line-906 contradiction and an overstated GPT-5.5 independence claim).

A rigor pass that started with Marcus refusing to draft the manuscript until each decomposition term was shown unbiased, drove a multi-step verification chase (variance reduction, analytical Hessians, expected-vs-sample RIV, then the one-RIV resolution), and ended by catching the Term-A-under-MAR non-cancellation and its likely Shimodaira precedent. Marcus's role was the standard-setter and skeptic: he posed the organizing question, demanded reproducibility and in-document checks, insisted simulations match the derivation, drove literature acquisition, and made every scope/framing call while the AI ran the compute.

Session executed the IC calibration-ladder arc (preregister rungs 1-3, run the new ladder script, land @sec-ic) and then Marcus zoomed out to audit the paper's overall standing. His role was strategic: he demanded reproducibility and verification evidence, asked what was actually proven and why it matters, supplied a Schafer normalization identity that explained the residual O(1) term, scoped the next session to a non-nested CS-vs-AR(1) comparison, and asked for the kickoff prompt.

Session pushing the deviance-bias result toward 'fully derived / fully pinned' for the (A)+(C) MAR term, with a standing cross-model verification gate. Marcus's role was to challenge the AI's framing that the (A)+(C) value was not load-bearing (it matters for LRT), audit whether the GPT-5.5 cross-check actually ran, stress-test the AI's MC-noise hypothesis by demanding a better simulation, enforce provenance and permanence on artifacts named 'scratch', and demand a multi-modality confidence verdict before deferring W1-W3 to a later session.

Marcus reframed the manuscript's credibility problem (a posture that presumes the reader's trust) and steered toward an IMRAD-with-explicit-Background structure, demanding concrete before/after examples before committing. He imposed a hard provenance rule (no weight-sourced citations; only manually-run Consensus searches feed the backlog) and posed a conceptual challenge about why preregistration matters for a deductive result. He then offloaded Background drafting to the AI while he ran literature searches, repeatedly asking for prose to be printed/rendered for phone reading.

A short session resuming the imputation-engine sensitivity screen (Amelia/EMB vs Bayes-proper data augmentation, todo/017), then pivoting to acquire and cite the Amelia (Honaker) and Tanner-Wong data-augmentation references. Marcus kicks it off with a detailed plan-of-record prompt, then catches a gap the AI missed (the engines are not Bayesianly proper in Schafer's sense even if sensitivity is not detected, so the manuscript should acknowledge it), and drives the litrev acquisition/citekey-fix loop.

A 2026-06-02 session running the known-variance experiment (todo/015) to decide whether the theorem's universality phrasing must be restated, then committing the C1/C3 scope-restatement result and accounting for what remained open (C2, C4, housekeeping). Marcus set the task with the decision gates driving scope, then mostly drove via short audit and process questions about commits, branch choice, and unresolved items, ending by asking for a handoff prompt to pick up C2 in a new session.

A short tail session following a derivation-audit run: the AI reported the audit was citation-clean at the blocker level with four reader-facing precision items. Marcus's role was record-keeping: he checked whether the audit report had been persisted, then directed it to a durable, untracked cache path with a timestamp, ensuring the audit verdict entered the project's reproducibility record rather than a transient temp file.

Session built the Sympy proof-verification notebook then pivoted to scaffolding the canonical sourced derivation (derivation.qmd) in stages: notation, ledger, full tagged proof. Marcus drove almost everything: he reframed the deliverable toward a fully formalized axiom/lemma registry, demanded equation- and line-level provenance for every step, controlled scope (stay MVN), interrogated notation choices, and rejected house jargon and symbol collisions. The AI executed and repeatedly surfaced decisions; Marcus's interrogation caught unsourced steps and a phi double-duty notation bug.

This session executed the non-nested (Vuong-regime) CS-vs-AR(1) measurement of the O(1) (A)+(C) differential (Proposition L4), the paper's only derived-but-never-measured claim, plus a follow-on dissimilar-pair run, and ended by rendering the manuscript and derivation to Google Drive. Marcus's role was to set the agenda via a detailed prereg-before-code handoff, supply compute-budget constraints, ratify the AI's proposed mid-run amendments, repeatedly demand an honest resolved/unresolved accounting, and approve the dissimilar-pair extension. He steered scope and epistemic standards while the AI did the derivation, runs, and drafting.

Session executed the general-p Cox-Snell b_Sigma derivation (todo/015) aiming to reproduce the empirical non-monotone (A)+(C) value of -0.46, but the magnitude bookkeeping did not close. Marcus's role was to probe the imputation methodology (Amelia vs conjugate data augmentation, burn-in, starting values), catch that the manuscript was speculating beyond what was derived, and spin the unresolved residual into cold-load hand-off plans (todo/017, todo/018) including a dual Claude+GPT-5.5 derivation requirement.

A single-turn operational session in which Marcus instructs the assistant to migrate Zotero-stored PDFs to Google Drive, part of the reference-acquisition pipeline that feeds literature/<citekey>.md. The human's role is purely directive: a logistics instruction with no conceptual, framing, or verification content.

Marcus resumed the C2 Delta_n entropy-reconciliation task (todo/017): prove analytically that the MVN Q-function bias equals the conditional-entropy plug-in bias and that the two decompositions are the same total regrouped, verified two ways (CAS + Monte Carlo). His opening turn was a fully specified work order with goal, two-way verification protocol, a pre-set decision gate, settled-facts guardrails, and a parallel-commit safety warning; the remaining turns steered toward updating the derivation qmd, ran a render, and audited where the reconciliation callout landed.

This session opened by tasking the AI with a Shimodaira positioning verdict (is the net tr(RIV) correction precedented, is the MAR (A)+(C) term genuinely beyond their framework), then moved into a plan-mode sequencing of the MAR derivation work, a reproducible phase-8 evidence run, and the general-MVN (A)+(C) write-up. Marcus's role was to set the agenda with a precise multi-part gate question, impose reproducibility discipline on temporary scripts, supply literature-verification routing (semantic scholar / pubmed / crossref), make compute and scope calls (R=40k run, option A), and spin out a side idea for a derivation-audit skill. He closed by directing a commit and a handoff prompt for the next session.

A logistics-and-handoff session (2026-06-01..02) around the closed-form (A)+(C) MAR term: Marcus migrates Zotero PDFs to Drive, hands off a literature-gated citation-swap task (Hyodo / Kanda-Fujikoshi for derivation.qmd sec-termA), runs a parallel derivation audit, and asks for a plan to address its findings plus a next-session prompt. The human role is overwhelmingly operational direction and process control: detailed task specification, citation discipline, and orchestrating a separate audit whose results feed the plan.

Marcus directed an independent adversarial re-derivation of the headline +1/2 tr(RIV) deviance-bias theorem: a 9-agent blind/method-diverse Layer-0/1 fan-out, a D9 definition fix, and a paid GPT-5.5 xhigh cross-model Layer-3 check. His role was to set the verification protocol, approve fixes, choose the cross-model engine and effort, and at the end rule that GPT's divergent findings be parked as conjectures for a fresh session rather than acted on immediately.

An end-to-end witness session (2026-05-23): drafting and preregistering the W1/W2/W3 simulation hypotheses (todo/004), implementing and running them, and then scoping out the comprehensive sweep (todo/005/006) with a fresh-session handoff. Marcus drove preregistration discipline, repeatedly redirected engine and parallelization choices, probed numerical results (W2 Type I, finite-N residuals), supplied applied-data domain knowledge (NHANES/HRS missingness), pasted external Consensus search output, and made the scope and design calls (p=4, MAR vs MNAR, M cap, monotone missingness, R=2000).

This session derived the W2 LRT-differential claims D1-D3 (H0 bias = tr(RIV_perp) in the I_com metric) through the full multimodal protocol (analytic-first, blind GPT-5.5 cross-model audit, simulation last), reconciled W1/H1, and landed the new @sec-lrt material in derivation.qmd. Marcus set the scope frame (W3 ladder parked as sequel) and the staged verification protocol up front, then ran an independent same-lineage audit of the derivation guide to close the one unchecked link (his own grading of the cross-model transcripts). Late in the session he pivoted toward the next session, contributing equipercentile/all-moment-matching domain judgment and driving the Kolen-Brennan literature acquisition pipeline.

This session executed the comprehensive simulation sweep (todo/005/006): W1/W3/H3b runs at R=2000, an MNAR parameter-bias measurement, a refactor into a single centralized run_all.R, and a CLAUDE.md/cleanup commit. Marcus's role was gatekeeper and architect: he enforced preregistration sign-off before running, demanded centralized reproducibility over scattered scripts, supplied the publishability judgment on which mechanisms and verifications were worth the compute, and opened a side thread on formal/Sympy proof verification.

This session drafted manuscript/mi-spectral.qmd from scratch by walking the argument DAG, with the human opening in plan mode and supplying the full drafting protocol: section-by-section map nodes, citation-discipline reading order, trust-ordered style constraints, and an explicit ask for the AI's recommendation on whether section 6 stands alone. After the draft, render, and review, the human's role narrowed to lightweight operational steers: render an HTML copy, audit commit state, and authorize the push to origin.

Marcus spun up a fresh, independent audit session for the just-landed W2 LRT-differential material (@sec-lrt, Propositions L1-L4) in derivation.qmd, instructing the auditor to treat every claim as suspect and re-derive the mathematics before reading the proofs. The single human turn is a fully specified verification protocol: derivation-audit, blind re-derivation, reproduction of the CAS/Monte-Carlo verification chain, an audit of the prior session's own cross-model grading, and an end-to-end H1 over-claim check, all under a strict scope fence. The human's role here is process architect: he designs an adversarial, independence-maximizing verification ritual rather than asking for new results.

Marcus reviews the canonical sourced derivation (derivation.qmd/html) and drives a wholesale restructuring for an outside theoretical-statistician reader: he demands a high-level roadmap, a proof sketch, and motivating prose around the bare algebra, and rejects house jargon ('ledge'/'numerical witness'/'consume'). His role is editorial and standard-setting plus quality control of fair-use handling of verbatim quotes; he repeatedly redirects to commit first and decides what stays vs. cuts.

Session enhances the provenance apparatus of manuscript/derivation.qmd per todo/008: a verbatim-source appendix organized by reference, Matrix Cookbook equation numbers, margin provenance blocks linked to appendix subsections, and integration of the sympy verification as Appendix B. Marcus's role was to set and tighten the citation-display standard (author/year must lead every Source line before any colon), require appendix linkage for every line-ref, approve retiring the standalone sympy file, and gate the commit/push.

An 8-hour session that designed the main manuscript's organization (full rewrite off the old spectral framing, derivation.qmd kept as a separate appendix-level proof) and drafted the front matter, a provenance/verification section, and sections 3-7 of mi-spectral.qmd. Marcus set the strategic frame (make the AI-derivation provenance visible to a skeptical reviewer, include Monte Carlo and honest failed predictions), reviewed each drafted chunk on the console, and through a confused probe about the Satorra-Bentler correction surfaced a real bug: the IC simulation applied SB as a standalone substitute rather than wrapping the bias correction. He then directed it be flagged in the draft, filed as a git issue (#1), noted in CLAUDE.md, and committed/pushed.

The 2026-06-04 session that turned the deferred W1-W3 empirical work-list into a strategic redesign. Through a long Socratic exchange Marcus pushed the AI from per-model bias correction toward an equipercentile-equating / moment-matching ladder for delta-AIC, then ruled the whole IC pairwise-matrix expansion out of this paper (scope split D-06, paper = bias + LRT), imposed an analytic-derivation-first + GPT-5.5 adversarial-audit gate before any full simulation, and drove the literature acquisition (Vuong, Barnard-Rubin, Self-Liang, Le Cam) to keep citation discipline intact. The human role was agenda-setting questioner, scope arbiter, process-standard setter, and resource driver.

A pre-drafting session on 2026-06-05: Marcus judged the manuscript ineffective and redirected the work to first build a concept map / argument DAG from the derivation guide, then acquire and verify a 'background' literature corpus (EM, imputation-as-Monte-Carlo, Rubin's rules as Laplace approximation) and a handoff for the drafting session. Marcus drove strategy (DAG ordering by node centrality, the background-for-trust rationale, banning businessy jargon) and personally ran the Zotero/litrev acquisition pipeline (migrate, convert, index, BBT export, Orchard-Woodbury year/key fixes), while the AI executed map construction, verification, and metadata reconciliation.

Working session executing the non-monotone (A)+(C) Cox-Snell pilot (todo/014): deriving the covariance-MLE second-order bias b_Sigma in Wolfram and checking it against the phase-8 empirical curve. Marcus opened with a detailed plan-of-record prompt with verification gates, then supervised the background runs, caught that the committed deliverable was only bivariate (not general-p), and steered the general-p / 4-variate -0.46 match into a fresh session with a handoff prompt.

episodes.quote: Let's keep it with a note that C4 has no empirical evidentiary basis yet, and is AI-hypothesized, but needs empriical validating. We deifintely want ot read CS very carefully
episodes.note: Types C4 as AI-hypothesized/unvalidated; mandates a careful Cavanaugh-Shumway read.
episodes.artifact: commit 715cd68 (2026-05-22 21:18 local; added literature/cavanaughAkaikeInformationCriterion1998a.md, ~74KB, and reframed the CS/AICcd parallel-universe comparison in IDEAS.md)
episodes.assessment: Quote is an exact verbatim match (typos included). The "read CS carefully" mandate is well-evidenced: commit 715cd68 the same session added the Cavanaugh-Shumway literature file and the CS/AICcd comparison in IDEAS.md. Caveat: the artifact_hint's "C4 conjecture typing" does not produce a literal C4/"AI-hypothesized" string in the committed record — IDEAS.md types the power-dominance claim as a "Conjecture" but no surviving C4 label; the CS-read move is the concrete, verifiable half.

episodes.quote: You need to write these in a markdown and commit and push for them to be preregistered
episodes.note: Imposes the rule that preregistration means committed and pushed, not just drafted.
episodes.artifact: commit ddc9037 (todo/004-simulation-hypotheses.md preregistration committed ~4 min after the turn)
episodes.assessment: Strong evidence for set-standard. Quote verbatim. The user's rule (prereg = markdown + commit + push) is enacted: commit ddc9037 "Preregister W1/W2/W3 simulation hypotheses" lands todo/004 (predictions written before code) at 03:18:38Z, ~4 min after the 03:14:37Z turn. artifact_hint correct.

episodes.quote:
CRITICAL FIRST STEP: confirm with me that I have signed off on todo/005.
The sweep hypotheses are written but marked PENDING. Do not run the
sweep until I explicitly confirm sign-off.
episodes.note: Resume prompt; hard-gates the sweep on explicit preregistration sign-off and forbids silent framework choices.
episodes.artifact: todo/005-comprehensive-sweep-hypotheses.md §4 sign-off ("Signed by marcus.waldman, 2026-05-23 ... Sweep execution per todo/006 begins"), committed in 5592d52
episodes.assessment: Quote verbatim-exact. The set-standard move (hard-gate sweep on explicit preregistration sign-off) is concretely evidenced: todo/005 §4 records the dated sign-off and "Sweep execution per todo/006 begins," committed (5592d52) ~11h after the gating turn. Correct ordering: prereg, gate, sign-off, then run. Strong fit.

episodes.quote: NOw it isn't enough to just cite the reference. You need to provide equations from the reference that you are invoking. If an equation is not available, provide the line numbber from the markdown.
episodes.note: Tightens provenance standard: every invoked source needs its equation or markdown line number.
episodes.artifact: commit 33c418b (Derivation: verbatim-source appendix + Matrix Cookbook eq numbers, todo 008)
episodes.assessment: Quote FOUND verbatim (user turn, incl. typos "NOw"/"numbber"). Standard realized same day: commit 33c418b builds a verbatim-source appendix giving each invoked source its own equation/theorem number AND literature/<citekey>.md line (e.g. Eq 520 at :3692; trace identities "cited by section + line" where no eq number exists) — exactly the episode's rule. todo/008 (commit 0703ebe) is the planning spec. artifact_hint's "check_provenance.py" never materialized (no such file); the discipline lives in the derivation/appendix, not a script.

episodes.quote: Yes, let's remove redundant citations.But we should make sure the author/apa citqation is clear. So like :2320 shouldn't just appear alone
episodes.note: Approves redundancy removal but tightens standard: a bare line-ref like :2320 must not stand alone without clear APA citation.
episodes.artifact: commit fb1408b (Derivation: drop redundant Verbatim links, attach line-refs as locators)
episodes.assessment: Strong match. Quote is verbatim. Turn at 10:27 MDT; commit fb1408b lands 12 min later, dropping redundant Verbatim pointers and adding a comma so van der Vaart (1998), :2320 reads as a locator, never bare — exactly the user's tightened standard. The named :2320 ref and vaart citekey appear in the diff.

episodes.quote: I'm not understanding what Anisotropic even means and wehther that is a terms that comes up in literature or something you invented?
episodes.note: Challenges the term anisotropic; asks if it is real literature or AI-invented jargon.
episodes.artifact: IDEAS.md line 96 — "anisotropy" demoted: "no longer the load-bearing claim, but it's still the setting"; term appears 0x in derivation.qmd and mi-spectral.qmd (introduced commit 715cd68)
episodes.assessment: Quote verbatim. Outcome verified: "anisotropic" is 0x in derivation.qmd (since 1st version) and mi-spectral.qmd, surviving only as a demoted descriptor in IDEAS.md. Caveat: the IDEAS.md demotion text (commit 715cd68, 21:18) predates the 23:40 challenge by ~2.3h, so the turn ratifies the demotion rather than literally producing it; no decisions.md entry (predates plan system).

episodes.quote: If C2 was .03 and C4 was .04 for the type 1 error, help me make sense of this statement the bias-corrected MI LRT controls Type I more precisely
episodes.note: Stress-tests the Type I claim against the actual numbers (.03 vs .04), forcing framing correction.
episodes.artifact: commit 40e7dac (IDEAS.md update with W1/W2/W3 empirical findings, 2026-05-23 09:45 -0600, ~21 min after the turn)
episodes.assessment: Genuine. Quote verbatim in turn 37. The challenge against near-equal Type I numbers forced the M-dependent calibration reframe, landed in IDEAS.md (40e7dac): C2=0.034/C3=0.042/C5=0.045, value is size-adjusted power not raw precision. Turn 40 ("we update IDEAS.md") confirms. Minor: final table labels C3 not C4.

episodes.quote: Why in D5 are you invoking Q^bar when defining the complete data likelihood? Y not just \scriptl(\theta|Y)?
episodes.note: Catches a definitional mismatch in D5; fix tightens I_com definition and Stage 3 convergence claim.
episodes.artifact: commit 1de7f40 (Derivation cleanup: Fisher-info definitions) — D5/D6 redefine I_com/I_obs as plain Fisher informations, "Removes the circular-looking bar-Q definition"; first-pass fix in immediately-preceding commit 1a6e601
episodes.assessment: Genuine. Pre-edit D5 literally read I_com = -d2 Q-bar/dt2; the challenge flagged the Q-bar-in-complete-likelihood mismatch. Two same-day commits ~12h later (1a6e601 first pass, 1de7f40 hint) replace it with plain Fisher-info form. Strong evidence.

episodes.quote: How did of perform in simulations with our bias correction. Remember Chan did not  correct bias he did his simulations
episodes.note: Probes whether SB was simulated with the bias correction; reminds that Chan never corrected the bias.
episodes.artifact: GitHub issue #1 "W3 (IC) SB arm: scaled-shift applied to raw deviance, should wrap the bias correction" (created 2026-05-25T12:12:10Z)
episodes.assessment: Quote verbatim (typos intact). Code "challenge-derivation" fits: Marcus probes whether SB was simulated with the bias correction and reminds Chan never corrected bias. Conversation flow (turns 14-15-18) leads directly to issue #1, created 22 min later, whose body encodes exactly this concern. artifact_kind=none since the schema lacks an "issue" option; issue is concrete and timing-matched.

episodes.quote: how are we evaluating the rate of decay? Are we fitting to a grid of n values and actually fitting a regression
episodes.note: Probes whether the decay rate is actually fitted; exposes that the rate was asserted, not measured.
episodes.artifact: commit 7e90166 (verification/remainder-rate.R, 2026-05-31 22:44, ~70 min after the turn)
episodes.assessment: Genuine. The probe directly produced verification/remainder-rate.R, which fits the decay exponent by WLS log-log regression on a log-spaced n grid (b=-1.34), and the commit notes the Term-A remainder is NOT MC-resolvable (asserted analytically), exactly the move described. Quote uuid was corrupted but the turn at the exact timestamp matches verbatim. artifact_hint's "todo/008" is loose, but the commit itself cites todo/008.

episodes.quote: Good lord. Can we not just do an analytic hessian?
episodes.note: Pushes back on slow numeric Hessian; demands an analytic Hessian for speed.
episodes.artifact: commit 6de630a (Analytic Fisher info for FIML engine, 230x speedup; replaces numDeriv numerical Hessian in verification/00-setup.R)
episodes.assessment: Strong evidence for redirect-priority. Quote is the entire turn, verbatim. Commit 6de630a lands 9 min after the turn, explicitly swapping the numDeriv numerical Hessian for closed-form analytic Fisher info (230x speedup) in the FIML engine, exactly the move demanded.

episodes.quote: I rather take a "knowledge accumulates and that shifts the document" than "lets get htis right in one shot"
episodes.note: Calls for plan mode and a sequential plan, accepting iterative rework over one-shot correctness.
episodes.artifact: commit 914bbf0 (Phase 3a: closed-form general-MVN (A)+(C) for monotone MAR)
episodes.assessment: Quote verbatim-FOUND with exact timestamp. episodes.quote: the fraction of information matrix has to be invertible. So do we need to make sure that we have a sufficient number of the amputations in order for the between amputation information component to be, um, uh, positive definite?

Present from the first derivation commit 33c53cf (2026-05-23 21:00, same session day).
episodes.assessment: Quote verbatim. Genuine catch-gap: surfaces the unstated FMI-invertibility assumption behind the RIV penalty. Landed as the R2 regularity condition (derivation.qmd, commit 33c53cf, same day). Caveat: R2 is the population I_com,I_obs > 0 condition; the finite-M floor the question literally raised was explicitly deferred to future work (next turn e651b204), not formalized.
